name: Expedia Group Rapid API
what_it_does: Expedia Group's Rapid API provides real-time hotel content, pricing, and booking capability for property partners and affiliate developers. Access to 700K+ properties worldwide.
use_cases: Building hotel search and booking flows in travel agent applications
Accessing real-time hotel availability and pricing for AI travel assistants
Hotel content management (photos, amenities, descriptions) for display
Rate shopping and competitive pricing analysis for hotel revenue managers
Multi-property booking management for travel agencies
not_for: General developers without a formal Expedia Group partner agreement
Consumer applications without proper OTA licensing
Small-volume integrations that don't justify partnership overhead
best_when: You are an approved Expedia Group partner building a hotel booking integration or travel application needing access to a massive global hotel inventory.
avoid_when: You need flights or are a general developer without an Expedia partnership — API access requires approval.

auth.notes: Authentication uses API key + secret for request signature. Signature is SHA-512 HMAC of key+secret+timestamp. More complex than simple Bearer token but manageable for server-side agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Test/sandbox access available during partner onboarding
pricing.paid_tiers: Commission-based or custom agreements through Expedia Group partnerships
pricing.notes: Commercial model varies by partner type — affiliate, technology partner, or direct supplier. Revenue share or commission models typical. Not self-service for general developers.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by partner agreement; SLA guaranteed

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include category and type fields. Some errors are hotel-inventory specific and require domain knowledge. Documentation is thorough but assumes hospitality industry knowledge.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Booking endpoints support client_token for idempotency. Search endpoints are read-only and inherently idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires formal Expedia Group partnership — cannot be used without approval
Authentication uses HMAC signature, not simple Bearer token — agents must implement signature logic
Property IDs in Expedia ecosystem differ from other systems — no universal property identifier
Rate availability expires — agents must book quickly after search
Content API and shopping API are separate systems with different auth flows
PCI compliance required if handling payment card data in booking flow
